uniformOmegaBranchTransportStatus.label: uniform omega branch transport
uniformOmegaBranchTransportStatus.reason: For |omega|<1/2, the branch factors exp(0.5 sigma omega(s+u)) are positive and locally uniformly controlled by the super-exponential theta/Phi decay in the Volterra representation.

omegaIndependentHardyMultiplierStatus.label: omega-independent Hardy multiplier contraction
omegaIndependentHardyMultiplierStatus.reason: The Green-lift closure theorem gives the completed-domain contraction for the multiplier kappa=(1-s-u)/(1+s+u), which is independent of omega.

quotientToOriginalLiftStatus.label: quotient-to-original Weyl lift
quotientToOriginalLiftStatus.reason: The quotient-to-original Weyl lift theorem transports the normalized Volterra quotient positivity back to the original coordinate Weyl kernel and includes primitive endpoint compatibility.

originalWeylPositivityStatus.label: original Weyl kernel positivity for |omega|<1/2
originalWeylPositivityStatus.reason: Uniform branch transport, the omega-independent Green contraction, and the quotient-to-original lift give positivity of K_omega for every |omega|<1/2.

proof: Use positive branch factors and theta/Phi decay for uniform omega branch transport.
Use the completed-domain Green-lift contraction for the omega-independent Hardy multiplier.
Use the quotient-to-original Weyl lift, including primitive endpoint compatibility.
Conclude original coordinate Weyl kernel positivity for every |omega|<1/2.
